title: Starbucks New Frappuccino Optimizer
subtitle: Constrained optimization for new Frappuccino product design
description:
## Model Summary

A constrained optimization model that finds the optimal nutritional and pricing attributes for a new Frappuccino product, given market conditions.

## How It Works

**Score = Desirability (0.35) × Affordability (0.35) × Seasonal Fit (0.30)**

### Desirability
- Calorie score: Gaussian centered at ~280 kcal (category median)
- Sugar score: Gaussian centered at ~42g
- Caffeine score: Baseline 0.7 + bonus for moderate caffeine (~100mg)

### Affordability
- Sigmoid on price z-score, adjusted by CPI and wage data

### Seasonal Fit
- Temperature sigmoid peaking above 65°F
- Monthly bonus: summer = 1.0, shoulder = 0.8, other = 0.4

## Optimization
- Method: L-BFGS-B with 20 random restarts
- Bounds: Calories 150-500, Sugar 20-65g, Caffeine 0-200mg, Price $4.00-$7.50

## Usage

```python
import pickle
with open('frappuccino_optimizer.pkl', 'rb') as f:
    model = pickle.load(f)
print(model['april_2026_optimal'])
# {'calories': 280, 'sugar_g': 42, 'caffeine_mg': 100, 'price': 4.89}
```

## Related Notebooks
- [New Frappuccino Optimizer](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-new-frappuccino-design-optimizer)
- [April 2026 Launch](https://www.kaggle.com/code/shiratoriseto/starbucks-april-2026-frappuccino-launch)

## Limitations
- Scoring weights are hand-designed, not learned from real sales data
- No flavor/taste modeling
- With real POS data, desirability weights could be calibrated
overview: Finds optimal calorie, sugar, caffeine, and price for new Frappuccino launches
